title: The One With The Rumor
weakest_section: 17:00-18:00 - Mrs. Altmann revelation section drags slightly

jokes.text: Hey, Rach, listen. Did you know that during pregnancy...your fingers swell up to twice their size and never go back?
jokes.setup: Monica delivers fake pregnancy 'fact' with serious tone
jokes.punchline: The absurd claim about permanent finger swelling
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the callback, creates expectation Rachel will believe it

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Did you know that when you're pregnant, your fingers swell up permanently? It's true. I read it on a website.

jokes.text: You fall for it every time.
jokes.setup: Rachel checking her fingers after the fake fact
jokes.punchline: Reveals Monica has been pranking Rachel repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and reveals ongoing dynamic. Strong payoff to setup

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, how many times have you done this?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, I don't know, maybe five or six times this month?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: FIVE OR SIX TIMES?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? You always fall for it!

jokes.text: And then after the baby's born, they're great for shoplifting melons.
jokes.setup: Innocent discussion of maternity clothes functionality
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's unexpected criminal application suggestion
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe - perfectly innocent delivery of absurd criminal suggestion. Highly quotable and character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know what's great about maternity clothes? The pockets are huge. You could totally shoplift a whole roasted chicken.

jokes.text: I mean, I was his thin friend.
jokes.setup: Monica describing Will as 'really overweight'
jokes.punchline: Monica positioning herself as the thin one despite her own past weight issues
jokes.explanation: Great character-based irony. Monica's obliviousness to her own past makes this land well

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Will was really overweight.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I mean, I was the thin one!

jokes.text: Are you sure you're not talking about your imaginary boyfriend? No, that was Jared.
jokes.setup: Ross questioning if Will existed
jokes.punchline: Monica matter-of-factly confirming she had a different imaginary boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery reveals Monica's childhood fantasy life. Economical and unexpected

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Will? Will Colbert?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I had an imaginary boyfriend too. His name was Chip Matthews.

jokes.text: [Monica clears throat after praising Will's looks]
jokes.setup: Monica gushing about how gorgeous Will looks now
jokes.punchline: Physical awkwardness as she realizes what she just said in front of Chandler
jokes.explanation: Standard physical beat for this type of situation, but well-placed

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Will, you look so gorgeous!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I mean... you look great. You really turned out... great.

jokes.text: I still love Chandler. - Wouldn't hurt you to say it once in a while.
jokes.setup: Monica's damage control after gushing about Will
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry commentary on their relationship dynamic
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor with Chandler's characteristic dry wit

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, I may have been a little enthusiastic about Will.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A little? You practically had his babies right there in front of us.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I did not!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh come on, you were glowing. I haven't seen you like that since... well, since you thought I was interesting.

jokes.text: No, they're not. They're ugly and stupid and delicious.
jokes.setup: Phoebe calling turkeys beautiful and intelligent
jokes.punchline: Joey's blunt counter-argument ending with 'delicious'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey response - simple, food-focused, and completely dismissive of sentiment

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Turkeys are beautiful and intelligent creatures.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Beautiful? Intelligent? Phoebe, they're delicious.

jokes.text: Or Friday with no two pizzas.
jokes.setup: Ross comparing Thanksgiving without turkey to July 4th without apple pie
jokes.punchline: Joey's personal Friday tradition as equivalent cultural touchstone
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly character-specific. Joey treats his personal eating habit as cultural tradition equal to national holidays

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey! Hey! What about Friday nights? That's my Thanksgiving!

jokes.text: That's right. Because I'm a Tribbiani. This is what we do.
jokes.setup: Challenge about eating a whole turkey
jokes.punchline: Joey invoking family pride for eating ability
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Joey's food challenge storyline. Family pride applied to eating is very Joey

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, I'm not just doing this for me. I'm doing this for my family. My father was a very big man, and my mother, she could really put it away. This is in my blood!

jokes.text: We may not be great thinkers or world leaders. We don't read a lot or run very fast, but damn it, we can eat.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining Tribbiani family identity
jokes.punchline: Litany of limitations followed by proud declaration of eating prowess
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and self-awareness. Joey's proud acceptance of family limitations except in eating. Very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm not a smart man. I'm not a good man. I'm not a strong man. I'm not a fast man. I'm not a rich man. But I'll tell you what I am – I'm a hungry man. And when Joey Tribbiani is hungry, Joey Tribbiani eats.

jokes.text: Rachel's. But good to know where you're at.
jokes.setup: Ross talking about 'a little baby at the table' while looking at Monica
jokes.punchline: Monica's dry clarification about whose baby and Ross's mindset
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan correction with subtle dig at Ross's self-centeredness

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: There's a little baby at the table.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's my baby, Ross.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: We're not at a barn dance.
jokes.setup: Monica correcting Ross's napkin folding technique
jokes.punchline: Specific critique suggesting his folding looks rustic/inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica perfectionism. The specific 'barn dance' reference makes it funnier than generic criticism

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ross, what is that? You're folding it all wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? It's a napkin.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's supposed to be a swan, Ross. A SWAN. Not whatever... farmyard animal that is.

jokes.text: Yeah, it all just came screaming back to me.
jokes.setup: Monica's reminder about Christmas swan napkin folding
jokes.punchline: Ross's sarcastic overstatement about remembering trivial detail
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcastic response to Monica's expectations about remembering napkin techniques

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh yes, the swan! How could I forget the most important Christmas tradition of all time?

jokes.text: I don't believe you. That is brilliant.
jokes.setup: Chandler revealing he's faking watching football to avoid helping
jokes.punchline: Ross's admiration for Chandler's deception
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Ross joining the scheme. His admiration rings true

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm not actually watching the football game. I'm just pretending so I don't have to help with the apartment.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's brilliant. You've mastered the art of strategic laziness.

jokes.text: Every once in a while I just yell and scream stuff at the TV.
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining how he fakes watching football
jokes.punchline: The simple method of randomly yelling at TV
jokes.explanation: Sets up the payoff when he demonstrates. Chandler's lazy but clever approach

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I just yell at the TV randomly. 'Come on!' 'That's a bad call!' 'What are you doing?!' Nobody knows the difference.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Anderson just scored again.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if his team is winning
jokes.punchline: Chandler making up a fake player name confidently
jokes.explanation: Good demonstration of his fake football watching method

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Is your team winning?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, we're up by three. Jamal Murray just hit a three-pointer.

jokes.text: There's no Anderson.
jokes.setup: Chandler's confident fake player reference
jokes.punchline: Ross matter-of-factly exposing the lie
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan correction. Ross's sports knowledge catching Chandler

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm a player. Women love me. I'm incredibly popular.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Chandler, you've never been on a date in your life.

jokes.text: Well, it's only like my favorite bay.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if Ross likes Green Bay
jokes.punchline: Ross treating 'Green Bay' literally as a body of water preference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literalist interpretation. Ross's academic nature makes this perfect character comedy

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Do you like Green Bay?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, it's a nice bay. I prefer the Caribbean, but Green Bay has its charm.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm gonna be in one of those Subway sandwich commercials.
jokes.setup: Monica noting Will lost 150 pounds
jokes.punchline: Reference to famous Subway weight loss ad campaign
jokes.explanation: Topical reference humor that was probably funnier when current. Serviceable but not special

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You lost 150 pounds!

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: I know. I'm like that Subway guy.

jokes.text: It's no good. Throw it out.
jokes.setup: Will's healthy pie description: no fat, no sugar, no dairy
jokes.punchline: Joey's immediate dismissal based on lack of unhealthy ingredients
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey response - if it's healthy, it's worthless. Economical and character-driven

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No fat, no sugar, no dairy? What's left? Newspaper?

jokes.text: Plus, I think it'd be better for my ego if we didn't stand right next to each other.
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining why he won't shake Will's hand
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment about Will's superior physical appearance
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler self-deprecation that acknowledges the visual without being overly harsh

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm not shaking his hand. Look at him, he's gorgeous. I'm not gonna shake his hand and have him think, 'Who's this guy? What's his deal?' No. I'm gonna stay over here, and he's never gonna know I exist.

jokes.text: Well done.
jokes.setup: Phoebe seeing Will's dramatic weight loss transformation
jokes.punchline: Treating weight loss like an achievement to be congratulated
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's unique way of acknowledging the transformation. Simple but effective

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Will! You lost so much weight!

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Yeah, I've been working out a lot.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That's amazing! You must feel so accomplished!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I mean, it's like you won an Olympic medal or something!

jokes.text: I'm watching the game but I'm not deaf.
jokes.setup: Monica and Will complimenting each other's appearances
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry reminder that he can hear them flirting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response to awkward situation. Dry and economical

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm still here, you know.

jokes.text: God, I hated her.
jokes.setup: Will learning Rachel is coming to dinner
jokes.punchline: Immediate, intense expression of hatred
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the high school backstory reveal. Appropriately uncomfortable

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: She's coming here? Tonight? Oh god, I... I can't... I hate her. I hate her so much.

jokes.text: Damn you, ref. You burn in hell!
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler deciding they need to yell at the TV
jokes.punchline: Ross's over-the-top sports anger performance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of fake sports watching. Ross overdoing it is character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We need to yell at the TV!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? Why?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Because that's what guys do! We yell at sports!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: COME ON, REF! THAT WAS A FOUL! ARE YOU BLIND?!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: UNBELIEVABLE! THE WORST CALL I'VE EVER SEEN!

jokes.text: Let me explain to you how the human body works. I have to warm up my stomach first. Eating chips is like stretching.
jokes.setup: Ross telling Joey to save room for turkey
jokes.punchline: Joey's pseudo-scientific explanation for pre-eating snacks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey logic applying athletic principles to eating. Creative and character-perfect

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey, you gotta save room for turkey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, I am. But I read that if you eat a little snack before a big meal, it actually makes you hungrier.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's not true.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, it is! It like, it shrinks your stomach lining, so it expands to take in more food.

jokes.text: And I'm here to tell you something. You can eat and eat and eat, but nothing will ever fill that void.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining Tribbianis never get full
jokes.punchline: Will's unexpectedly deep psychological commentary on overeating
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly profound moment from Will that contrasts with Joey's simple food joy

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Tribbianis never get full.

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: That's because eating is just a symptom. The real hunger is emotional—we're all just trying to fill the void inside ourselves with food.

jokes.text: Hot stuff?
jokes.setup: Will calling Ross 'hot stuff' enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Ross's confused/uncomfortable reaction to the pet name
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Will's overeager friendliness with Ross

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Hot stuff!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Uh... thanks?

jokes.text: Would've been good if we had gotten in, but still, real fun.
jokes.setup: Will mentioning Lance Davis' graduation party as fun
jokes.punchline: Ross revealing they weren't actually invited/didn't get in
jokes.explanation: Good reveal about their high school social status. Ross's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Lance Davis' graduation party was so fun!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We weren't actually invited. We snuck in through the bathroom window.

jokes.text: God, we were lame back then. Do you remember how into dinosaurs we were?
jokes.setup: Will and Ross reminiscing about high school
jokes.punchline: Will calling their shared interest lame, not realizing Ross still loves dinosaurs
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment - Will doesn't realize Ross is still passionate about dinosaurs

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Remember when we were obsessed with dinosaurs?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, those were great times.

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Man, that was so lame. I can't believe we were into that.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: ...I still am.

jokes.text: Ross avoiding answering what he does now after Will calls dinosaurs lame
jokes.setup: Will asking what Ross does now after dismissing dinosaur interest
jokes.punchline: Ross deflecting rather than admitting he's a paleontologist
jokes.explanation: Perfect uncomfortable beat showing Ross's embarrassment about his career choice

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: So what do you do now?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm a paleontologist.

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Still?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes, still. Why does everyone say it like that?

jokes.text: Oh, I'll be fine. It's just... God, I hate her, Ross. I hate her.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if Will will be okay seeing Rachel
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate return to intense hatred after claiming he'd be fine
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction showing Will can't actually let go of his high school resentment

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Will, are you going to be okay seeing Rachel?

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, I'll be fine.

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: I'm completely over it.

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: It's not a problem at all.

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: I mean, the hatred I have for her still burns deep inside me, but I think I can manage for one evening.

jokes.text: My two greatest enemies, Ross: Rachel Green and complex carbohydrates.
jokes.setup: Will staring at Rachel with the yams
jokes.punchline: Treating food group as equal enemy to specific person
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line putting personal grudge and diet restriction on same level. Highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: I declare hatred on all yams.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? Why?

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Because you're the enemy, and yams are the enemy, so now they're equal.

jokes.text: Wow, really got that sexy smoldering thing going on.
jokes.setup: Rachel seeing Will staring intensely at her
jokes.punchline: Misinterpreting his hateful stare as seductive
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - audience knows he hates her but she thinks he's attracted

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Why is he looking at me like that?

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: I'm not looking at you any particular way.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, you are. You're looking at me like... like you want me.

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: I don't want you.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, stop it. You're making me blush.

jokes.text: [Will mouths 'I hate you' while Rachel thinks he's flirting]
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to read what Will is mouthing to her
jokes.punchline: Visual of Will clearly mouthing hatred while she expects romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy showing the complete miscommunication between characters

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What are you saying? I can't read lips.

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: I hate you.

jokes.text: Well, the Lions technically won. But it was a moral victory for the Green Bay Mermen.
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler giving different answers about who won the game
jokes.punchline: Ross's made-up team name and nonsensical explanation
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the bay joke while showing how badly Ross fakes sports knowledge

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: The Gellers won! That's my family team.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, the Gellers? That's not even a real team.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes it is! They won 47 to... purple.

jokes.text: Did we, um...? Did we fool around at Lance Davis' graduation party?
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to remember Will from high school
jokes.punchline: Suggesting romantic encounter at party they didn't even attend
jokes.explanation: Good callback and shows Rachel's self-centered assumption about past interactions

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Will! Yes! I remember you from high school!

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: You do?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Of course! Didn't we make out at that party in 1987?

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Rachel, you weren't even at that party.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, maybe it was a different party then.

jokes.text: You are unbelievable.
jokes.setup: Rachel's assumption they hooked up
jokes.punchline: Will's disgusted response to her narcissistic assumption
jokes.explanation: Good response showing Will's continued resentment, though not particularly original

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You hooked up with Monica?

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: No! I mean, come on, that was like... no. You know what? I'm not even going to dignify that with a response.

jokes.text: It's like me when I was born.
jokes.setup: Learning the turkey weighs 19 pounds
jokes.punchline: Joey comparing turkey weight to his birth weight
jokes.explanation: Good Joey self-reference that's both self-deprecating and explains his large appetite

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Nineteen pounds? That's how much I weighed when I was born!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, that's not possible.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? My mother was a very large woman!

jokes.text: Queen Rachel, does whatever she wants in her little Rachel-land.
jokes.setup: Will calling Rachel's food request 'typical'
jokes.punchline: Will's bitter characterization of Rachel's perceived entitlement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Will's resentment with creative phrasing of his perception

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Of course you want the best turkey. That's so typical of you, Rachel. You know what? I have spent the last ten years perfecting my turkey and I'm not about to let it go to waste on someone like you.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Excuse me?

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: You heard me. You waltz in here, probably expecting the best of everything, without ever having to work for anything in your life.

jokes.text: Screw it. Bring on the yams.
jokes.setup: Monica reminding Will he's worked hard on his diet
jokes.punchline: Will abandoning his diet in emotional reaction to Rachel
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how Rachel triggers Will's destructive impulses

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Will, you've worked so hard on your diet.

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: I know, I know. But... it's Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: All those years, Monica. All those years of... I can't do this anymore.

jokes.text: Me and Ross.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking who else was in the 'I Hate Rachel' club
jokes.punchline: Revealing Ross was Will's co-conspirator
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff. Simple delivery makes the revelation more shocking

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Ross.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross?!

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Yeah, he was the vice president.

jokes.text: No need to point. She knows who Ross is.
jokes.setup: Will presumably pointing at Ross after naming him
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily clarifying the obvious for comedic effect
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan humor pointing out the obvious in an awkward moment

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: That's Ross. He's the one I hate.

jokes.text: You went out with her? We had a pact.
jokes.setup: Learning Ross and Rachel dated
jokes.punchline: Will's sense of betrayal over breaking their hate pact
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Will took their high school club seriously as an adult commitment

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: You guys dated? You broke the pact!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What pact?

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: The 'I Hate Rachel Green Club' pact. We made it in high school!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: There was a club?

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Yes! And you were the president!

jokes.text: Then why did it have the word 'eternity' in it?
jokes.setup: Ross saying the pact wasn't binding forever
jokes.punchline: Revealing they put 'eternity' in their high school hate pact
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing how seriously they took their teenage club. Absurd but perfectly logical to Will

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: The pact wasn't binding forever.

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Actually, we put 'eternity' in the high school hate pact.

jokes.text: A little relieved, I gotta say.
jokes.setup: Learning the boys locked themselves in Ross's room for club meetings
jokes.punchline: Monica's relief it wasn't something sexual
jokes.explanation: Good Monica response showing what she had been thinking about the secret meetings

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh thank God. For a second there I thought you guys were doing something sexual.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We could if you'd leave.

jokes.text: Cofounded.
jokes.setup: Ross saying he 'joined' the club
jokes.punchline: Will correcting the record about their equal partnership
jokes.explanation: Perfect pedantic correction showing Will's pride in their joint venture

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: You didn't join the club. We founded this club together. It was an equal partnership.

jokes.text: Uh, except that it was really the 'I Hate Rachel Club.'
jokes.setup: Ross's romantic reframing of their club as really being about love
jokes.punchline: Will's literal correction bringing it back to the actual hatred
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Ross's attempt to romanticize their teenage cruelty

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: The I Hate Rachel Green Club was really about love.

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: No, it was about hating Rachel Green.

jokes.text: ...you had both male and female reproductive parts.
jokes.setup: Building up to revealing what rumor they started
jokes.punchline: The hermaphrodite rumor revelation
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the rumor setup, appropriately shocking and specific

jokes.text: We said your parents flipped a coin...decided to raise you as a girl, but you still had a hint of a penis.
jokes.setup: The hermaphrodite rumor revelation
jokes.punchline: Specific elaborate details about the fake condition
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with specific absurd details. 'Hint of a penis' is particularly memorable phrasing

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: There was a rumor that I was a hermaphrodite.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Yeah, in high school there was this rumor that I was a hermaphrodite.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No!

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Yeah. I was in the locker room and I was changing and apparently I didn't close the curtain all the way and someone saw me and started this rumor that I was a hermaphrodite.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Yeah, it was terrible. It got around the whole school. People would come up to me in the hallway like, 'Hey Will, is it true?' And I would have to be like, 'No, it's not true.'

jokes.text: You were the hermaphrodite cheerleader?
jokes.setup: Learning about the rumor Ross and Will started
jokes.punchline: Phoebe recognizing Rachel as a famous rumor she'd heard
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing the rumor spread beyond their school. 'Hermaphrodite cheerleader' is perfectly specific

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God! You're the girl from the rumor!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: In high school, there was this rumor that some girl did it with her entire soccer team in the woods?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh honey, I'm sorry. I mean, you were a legend!

jokes.text: Actually, Billy Tratt is gay now, so that one's not really our fault.
jokes.setup: Rachel blaming the rumor for Billy Tratt's behavior
jokes.punchline: Ross's matter-of-fact correction about why Billy acted that way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross logic - he can't let an incorrect assumption stand even in this context

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I mean, he was probably just reacting to the rumor.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, he was reacting to the fact that you told him you'd sleep with him if he shaved his head.

jokes.text: I thought it might be true. And I was afraid that you were gonna cry and then show it to me.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking why Monica never told her about the rumor
jokes.punchline: Monica's admission she believed it and feared an awkward reveal
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly uncomfortable admission. Monica's specific fear about crying and showing is perfectly awkward

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, you believed it? Why didn't you ever tell me?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Because I was afraid! I thought you'd be upset, and then it would be this whole thing where we'd have to talk about it, and I didn't know how to bring it up without making it weird.

jokes.text: I'm afraid I'm gonna need proof.
jokes.setup: Rachel insisting there's nothing there
jokes.punchline: Joey's inappropriate request for physical evidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey response - completely inappropriate but delivered with innocent curiosity

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: There's nothing there, Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh come on, there's gotta be something. Can I see?

jokes.text: You are my Everest.
jokes.setup: Joey looking at the turkey he needs to finish
jokes.punchline: Treating the turkey as his ultimate challenge to conquer
jokes.explanation: Iconic Joey line. Perfect metaphor applying mountaineering to food consumption. Highly quotable and character-perfect

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: This is it. This is the one. This turkey... this is my Everest.

jokes.text: Today I'm just a guy who can't finish a turkey...but tomorrow I'm the guy who eats half a Power Bar...wraps up the rest and puts it in the fridge? No.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining why he must finish the turkey
jokes.punchline: Specific fear of becoming someone who saves half a Power Bar
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey philosophy. The specific Power Bar example perfectly captures his food relationship fears. Near-perfect character comedy

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I have this specific fear of becoming someone who saves half a Power Bar.

jokes.text: Sorry about your teeny weenie.
jokes.setup: Rachel reading yearbook message that started normally
jokes.punchline: Yearbook message confirming the rumor's impact
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the rumor's widespread impact through yearbook evidence

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: "Dear Rachel, you have always been so nice to everyone. I hope your dreams come true. Your friend, Sean McMahon." Well, that's sweet. Oh wait, there's more. "P.S. - Just kidding, I hope you choke."

jokes.text: Well, you did start that rumor about Ross...making out with Mrs. Altmann, our 50-year-old librarian.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming he never hurt Rachel in high school
jokes.punchline: Monica revealing Rachel's revenge rumor about Ross
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and setup for the bigger reveal that it was true

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, you never hurt Rachel? That's funny, because she told me you used to follow her around the mall.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? That's not true!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And she said you wrote her name in your yearbook 47 times.

jokes.text: Yes, it is. I saw you guys going at it behind the card catalog.
jokes.setup: Ross denying the librarian rumor
jokes.punchline: Phoebe confirming she witnessed it actually happening
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - what seemed like Rachel's revenge rumor was actually true. Great plot twist

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I was not in the library with the librarian!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I was there, Ross. I saw the whole thing. You two were going through the card catalog together.

jokes.text: She also made out...with Ta-Taka-Ki-Kek the night before he went back to Thailand.
jokes.setup: Revealing Mrs. Altmann's inappropriate behavior with Ross
jokes.punchline: Adding the Thai exchange student to her conquest list
jokes.explanation: Good callback and escalation showing Mrs. Altmann was predatory with multiple students

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, so Mrs. Altmann was... with Ross?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, and apparently she's added the Thai exchange student to her list.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's so inappropriate!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know! She's like, ancient!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, I mean... she's getting more action than all of us combined.

jokes.text: She probably wasn't familiar with the process...having spent most of her life sitting for oil paintings.
jokes.setup: Monica saying Mrs. Altmann didn't photograph well
jokes.punchline: Chandler's elaborate age joke about pre-photography era
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler elaborate age joke. Creative historical reference makes it more than just 'she's old'

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, maybe she's just old. I mean, she could be from before they invented the camera. She might've been like, 'Hey, what's that thing? I don't trust it.' And then she got older and cameras got better, but by then she was already set in her ways.

jokes.text: She needed some help with her word jumble. And one thing led to another.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining how the encounter with Mrs. Altmann started
jokes.punchline: Innocent activity leading to inappropriate outcome
jokes.explanation: Good understated explanation of seduction. Word jumble as foreplay is appropriately nerdy for Ross

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It started completely innocently. We were just going over some files in my office.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And then... well, one thing led to another.

jokes.text: If you must know, Anita was very gentle and tender. May she rest in peace.
jokes.setup: Ross defending his relationship with Mrs. Altmann
jokes.punchline: Romantic description followed by death confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good uncomfortable moment with Ross's genuine fondness for the inappropriate relationship

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: She's a wonderful woman. We have a real connection. She's intelligent, she's cultured, she's...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Dead?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes.

jokes.text: Only when it was damp.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking if Mrs. Altmann walked with a cane
jokes.punchline: Ross's specific knowledge about her mobility issues
jokes.explanation: Good detail showing Ross's intimate knowledge of his elderly lover's condition

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Did Mrs. Altmann walk with a cane?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, she did. She had a bad hip from a skiing accident in 1987.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I'm sorry. I never got to be in a club. I didn't go to high school. But three of us would meet behind a Dumpster to learn French.
jokes.setup: Phoebe wanting to join the 'I Hate Rachel' club
jokes.punchline: Her street education alternative to high school clubs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe backstory. Dumpster French lessons perfectly captures her unconventional education

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I want to join the 'I Hate Rachel' club. On the street, we had clubs too. We had the 'I Hate Phoebe' club. I was president.

jokes.text: One girl wanted to be like you so much, she stuffed her pants with a Tootsie Roll.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining how popular Rachel was despite the rumor
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd method another girl used to emulate the rumor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd detail. Tootsie Roll as fake appendage is both specific and ridiculous. Highly quotable

jokes.text: As a romancer of the elderly.
jokes.setup: Monica saying Rachel's rumor put Ross 'on the map'
jokes.punchline: Monica's flowery description of Ross's reputation
jokes.explanation: Good elegant phrasing that makes Ross's reputation sound almost distinguished

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, thanks to that rumor, Ross is finally on the map!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He's the guy all the girls want to... you know, with the paleontology and the adorable awkwardness...

jokes.text: So you knocked her up but you're not gonna marry her. Dude.
jokes.setup: Learning Ross got Rachel pregnant but they're not marrying
jokes.punchline: Will's old-fashioned moral judgment of Ross
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's traditional values judging modern relationship

dialogue.speaker: Will
dialogue.line: Ross got Rachel pregnant? And they're not getting married? What is wrong with you people?

jokes.text: Joey, those are my maternity pants.
jokes.setup: Joey wearing expandable pants for his eating challenge
jokes.punchline: Revealing he borrowed Rachel's pregnancy clothes
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback and practical Joey solution to his eating challenge

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: These pants are amazing! I can eat whatever I want and they just... expand!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, those are my maternity pants!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, that explains why they fit so well. Who knew pregnancy clothes were designed for eating competitions?

jokes.text: No, these are my Thanksgiving pants.
jokes.setup: Rachel identifying her maternity pants on Joey
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact renaming for his purpose
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - he's redefined the pants by their new function. Economical and character-perfect

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Those are my maternity pants!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Not anymore. They're my Thanksgiving pants.

jokes.text: Here come the meat sweats.
jokes.setup: Joey finishing his turkey eating challenge
jokes.punchline: Specific physical consequence of massive meat consumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey aftermath. 'Meat sweats' is specific and visceral enough to be memorable

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I can't feel my stomach anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's not a good sign, Joe.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, it's great! It means I can keep eating!

jokes.text: Just cut me a little sliver. A little bigger. Little bigger. What, are you afraid you're gonna run out? Cut me a real piece.
jokes.setup: Joey asking for pie after eating entire turkey
jokes.punchline: Progressively demanding bigger pieces despite being full
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Joey's food relationship. Despite being painfully full, he can't accept a small piece

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm stuffed, but I could use a little piece of pie.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, you just ate an entire turkey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So? I got room for pie. Give me a bigger piece.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, no.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Come on, I'm still hungry. Make it bigger.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Actually, you know what? Make it even bigger than that.

tmdb_overview: Monica invites Will, an old high school friend of her and Ross over for a Thanksgiving dinner, unaware that he isn't too fond of Rachel. Meanwhile, Joey vows to eat an entire turkey by himself, and Chandler and Phoebe pretend to watch a football game in order to avoid helping in the kitchen.
insight: Thanksgiving special sustains 88.8 score across 73 jokes with escalating character chaos.
